briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome. We will now begin the climate briefing for June 24, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Global Climate Crisis: Extreme Weather and Urgent Warnings
sections.content: Globally, the climate crisis is intensifying, leading to unprecedented extreme weather phenomena. In Europe, record-breaking heatwaves exceeding 40 degrees Celsius have resulted in casualties and the cancellation of climate change events in London [25, 172, 176, 178, 197, 205]. Domestically, the World Cup was affected by heatwaves and thunderstorms, leading to increased carbon emissions, and warnings have been issued that the foundation of Jeju's agriculture is being shaken [1, 7, 46]. These phenomena are so severe that what was once considered 'abnormal weather' is now recognized as the 'new normal climate,' posing a serious threat to public health, with the risk of death increasing by 11% when heatwaves coincide with ozone [28, 40]. The UN Secretary-General urged increased financial support for climate change adaptation, emphasizing that climate change is an interconnected issue with housing crises and gender inequality, alongside concerns about rising utility and healthcare costs [184, 188, 201]. Furthermore, Olympic gold medalists warned that climate change threatens future Winter Olympics and called for solutions [180, 183, 195, 200].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Strengthening Domestic and International Policies and Cooperation: Seeking Accelerated Climate Action
sections.content: Domestic and international policy cooperation to address the climate crisis is being actively discussed. The Ambassador for Climate Change of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs attended the 10th Ministerial Meeting on Climate Action (MoCA10) in Brussels, where discussions focused on strengthening the implementation of the Paris Agreement and accelerating global climate action [2, 12, 26, 27]. Domestically, local governments in the Chungcheong region, including Chungnam, Daejeon, and Sejong, are establishing regional cooperation systems by holding joint forums on climate crisis response, strengthening carbon neutrality cooperation, and exploring science-based joint response measures [4, 14, 20, 21, 24, 29, 30, 31, 33, 34, 35, 36, 41, 42, 43, 45, 91, 94, 99, 108, 115, 121, 130, 132]. Furthermore, international cooperation strategies to achieve both energy security and carbon neutrality were discussed in Jeju [60, 65, 82, 86, 87, 101], and the UN Secretary-General emphasized climate responsibility in the technology sector, urging AI companies to disclose the environmental impact of their data centers [6, 18]. In Mongolia, discussions on climate change legislation are expected to expand green finance and carbon markets, broadening the scope of international climate response [13].

sections.title: 🌱 Expanding Innovative Technologies and Public-Private Cooperation for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Technological innovation and public-private cooperation are expanding across the board to transition towards a carbon-neutral society. The Korea Land and Housing Corporation (LH) presented a blueprint for future housing at the '2026 Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport Technology Fair,' showcasing carbon-neutral future city technologies, including zero-energy homes and delivery robots [56, 57, 58, 61, 64, 71, 74, 75, 76, 79, 80, 84, 85, 90, 96, 98, 102, 103, 113, 117, 119]. Pohang City has secured a 43.1 billion KRW national carbon-neutral project to establish the nation's first 'DAC-RCC demonstration complex,' officially beginning its transformation into a climate tech city [47, 48, 49, 63, 88, 110, 126]. LG Chem has commenced demonstration of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) utilizing carbon dioxide (CO₂), aiming to secure 'flying carbon neutrality' technology [77, 81]. Concurrently, Hadong-gun and Anseong City are encouraging citizen participation by operating 'Carbon Neutrality Point System' and 'Climate Action Opportunity Income' programs, which provide incentives for carbon-neutral practices [22, 51, 52, 68, 69, 70, 89, 109, 118, 122, 125, 129]. Seongnam City is running a 'Carbon Neutrality Living Lab' with direct citizen participation to conduct experiments on reducing greenhouse gas emissions in daily life [53, 54, 55, 95, 106, 107, 112, 120, 131].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Efforts for Grid Stabilization
sections.content: As efforts to establish renewable energy as a primary power source in the electricity market accelerate, the importance of grid stabilization is also being highlighted. 'Mohhaet,' a renewable energy investment platform, has surpassed 500 billion KRW in cumulative investments, demonstrating the revitalization of private investment [136, 145, 170]. Kakao announced in its '2025 Sustainability Report' that it increased its renewable energy procurement by 2.8 times compared to the previous year and reduced data center greenhouse gas emissions by 4,753 tons [39, 143, 146, 154, 155, 160, 166]. Meanwhile, to address concerns about grid instability due to the expansion of renewable energy, the government plans to significantly enhance power stability by fully introducing 'grid-forming' technology into Energy Storage Systems (ESS) [134, 139, 142, 152, 169]. However, alongside rebuttals to the claim that 'more renewable energy leads to more power outages,' calls have also been made for a societal discussion on the 'appropriate level of renewable energy use,' illustrating the dilemma of energy transition [137, 140, 141, 144]. Regional cooperation for renewable energy transition is also active, with Jeonbuk Development Corporation and KIST signing an MOU for direct solar power supply [147, 148, 157, 159, 165].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has once again underscored the importance of international cooperation, domestic innovation efforts, and accelerating the renewable energy transition, even amidst the deepening climate crisis. Continued attention and action are essential for responding to climate change. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_title: CCC: Faster electrification of UK will ‘put money back into people’s pockets’
articles.description: Faster electrification is the best way to secure lower energy bills and stronger energy security,... The post CCC: Faster electrification of UK will ‘put money back into people’s pockets’ appeared first on Carbon Brief.

summary.phenomenon: The UK Climate Change Committee (CCC) has advocated for faster electrification in the UK.
summary.cause: This assertion is based on the premise that faster electrification is the best way to lower energy bills and strengthen energy security.
summary.outlook: Should faster electrification be implemented, it is projected that energy bills for the UK public will decrease, energy security will be enhanced, and consequently, financial savings will accrue to citizens.
